ROM 9:28.notes: Closes the Isa 10:22-23 LXX quotation (continuing from v.27). 'The Lord will execute His word completely and decisively on the earth.' Final-judgment language.

ROM 9:29.notes: Direct quote of Isaiah 1:9 LXX. The 'Lord of Hosts' (κύριος Σαβαώθ — Hebraism preserved in LXX) preserves a remnant — without which Israel would have shared Sodom's fate. Sodom and Gomorrah (Gen 19) as the divine-judgment-exemplar.

ROM 9:33.notes: Composite-quotation fusing Isaiah 28:16 LXX + Isaiah 8:14 LXX. Isa 28:16 (positive — believer-not-shamed) + Isa 8:14 (negative — stone-of-stumbling). Paul reads the SAME-stone as both — depending on response. Same composite-text used at 1 Pet 2:6-8 — shared early-Christian Stone-Christology tradition.

ROM 9:3.notes: Echoes Exodus 32:32 LXX — Moses' offer to be 'blotted-out' from God's book for Israel's-sake after the golden-calf incident. Paul echoes Moses' shepherd-heart in his ἀνάθεμα-wish: willing to be cursed for the sake of his Jewish kinsmen. Hyperbolic-rhetorical, not literally-attainable, but conveys the depth of pastoral-love.

ROM 10:5.notes: Direct quote of Leviticus 18:5 LXX (the do-and-live formula of Mosaic-Law). Same OT-text Paul cites at Gal 3:12 to-show Law-righteousness operates by do-and-live (vs faith-righteousness which-receives-and-lives). Pauline-Lev 18:5 = the works-Law-paradigm-text.

ROM 10:6.notes: Direct quote of Deuteronomy 30:12 LXX (with Pauline-Christological-application). In context, Moses argues that the Law is NOT-far-away (need-not-ascend-to-heaven). Paul applies to faith-righteousness with midrashic-gloss 'that-is, to-bring-Christ-down.' Pauline-creative-rereading of Moses-as-pointing-to-Christ.

ROM 10:7.notes: Adapted-quote of Deuteronomy 30:13 LXX. Original LXX has 'who-will-cross-the-sea?'; Paul substitutes ἄβυσσος 'abyss / depths-of-earth' for the sea-crossing — applying the underworld-of-death image for Christ's-resurrection. Pauline-creative-rereading.

ROM 10:8.notes: Direct quote of Deuteronomy 30:14 LXX. Closes Pauline-midrash on Deut 30:12-14. Moses' near-word becomes Pauline-near-Christ-word: the gospel is not far-away but accessible, in mouth and heart.

ROM 10:11.notes: Re-cite of Isaiah 28:16 LXX (cf 9:33). Paul adds πᾶς ('every-one') to make universality-explicit, opening the door for v.12's Jew-Gentile-equality-statement.

ROM 10:13.notes: Direct quote of Joel 2:32 LXX (Joel 3:5 LXX). The κύριος in Joel-LXX = YHWH; Paul applies it Christologically (continuous with v.9's κύριον Ἰησοῦν) — Christ-as-κύριος receives the prayer-formerly-directed-to-YHWH. Foundation-text for Christian-prayer-to-Jesus. Acts 2:21 cites the same Joel passage with same Christological application.

ROM 10:15.notes: Direct quote (compressed) of Isaiah 52:7 LXX. Original-LXX has 'feet-of-the-one-bringing-good-news-of-peace, the-one-bringing-good-news-of-good-things'; Paul condenses to 'feet-of-good-news-bringers.' The famous-feet-of-evangelists text. Foundational-mission-text.

ROM 10:16.notes: Direct quote of Isaiah 53:1 LXX. The opening of the famous-suffering-servant-passage. Paul applies Isaiah's-lament about-Israel's-unbelief in the gospel-message. The ἀκοή ('what-is-heard / message') becomes the gospel-report.

ROM 10:18.notes: Direct quote of Psalm 19:4 LXX (Ps 18:5 LXX). Originally about creation-as-universal-witness; Paul re-applies to gospel's-universal-proclamation. Pauline-creative-application of natural-revelation Psalm to special-revelation gospel-spread.

ROM 10:19.notes: Direct quote of Deuteronomy 32:21 LXX (Moses' Song-of-witness). God's prophetic-warning that-disobedient-Israel will-be-provoked-to-jealousy by-a-no-people. Paul applies: Gentile-conversion is the means-of-Israel's-jealousy. Theme developed in ch.11.

ROM 10:20.notes: Direct quote of Isaiah 65:1 LXX. God-revealed-himself-to-those-not-seeking — Pauline-application: Gentile-conversion. Paul calls Isaiah's-statement ἀποτολμάω 'bold-speech' (NT-HAPAX) — provocative-prophetic-revelation.

ROM 10:21.notes: Direct quote of Isaiah 65:2 LXX. God's-pleading-posture toward-unfaithful-Israel ('all-day-stretched-out-hands'). Closes ROM 10 with divine-pathos that prepares-for-ch.11's-restoration-question. Counter-balances v.20's Gentile-find-by-non-seekers with v.21's Israel-rejection-of-pleading-God.

ROM 11:3.notes: Direct quote of 1 Kings 19:10/14 LXX (Elijah's complaint to God on Mount Horeb after fleeing Jezebel). Pauline-Elijah-typology: just as Elijah-thought-himself-alone but God-had-7000, so Paul-may-think-Israel-rejected but God-has-the-remnant.

ROM 11:4.notes: Quote of 1 Kings 19:18 LXX (paraphrased — Pauline-active-voice 'I-have-reserved' vs LXX-passive-future 'will-leave'). The remnant-doctrine in OT-form: 7000 not bowing to Baal.

ROM 11:8.notes: Composite-quotation fusing Deut 29:4 LXX + Isa 29:10 LXX. Both texts speak of Israel's-dulled-perception (eyes-not-seeing, ears-not-hearing). Paul fuses them to characterize Israel's-current-hardening.

ROM 11:9.notes: Direct quote of Psalm 69:22-23 LXX (Ps 68:23-24 LXX). Imprecatory-Psalm-of-David applied to Israel's-rejection. Four-fold catalog (snare/trap/stumbling/retribution) followed by darkened-eyes + bent-back in v.10.

ROM 11:10.notes: Continues Psalm 69:23 LXX (Ps 68:24 LXX). συγκάμπτω 'bend-down-with' is NT-HAPAX.

ROM 11:26.notes: Adapted-quote of Isaiah 59:20 LXX. Original-LXX: 'come on-account-of-Zion'; Paul has 'come from-Zion' (substantial-shift; Pauline-creative-rereading). Combined with Isa 59:21 in v.27. Eschatological-Christ-coming-from-Zion to deliver-Jacob.

ROM 11:27.notes: Composite-quotation: Isa 59:21 LXX + Jer 31:33-34 LXX (= Jer 38:33-34 LXX) (with summary-paraphrase of new-covenant-promise). 'My-covenant... when-I-take-away-their-sins' — Pauline-eschatological-covenant fulfillment in Israel's-restoration.

ROM 11:34.notes: Direct quote of Isaiah 40:13 LXX. 'Who has known the mind of the Lord?' Same Isaiah-text Paul cites at 1 Cor 2:16. The unsearchable-divine-mind theme. Begins the closing-doxology of Rom 1-11.

ROM 11:35.notes: Quote of Job 41:11 LXX (Hebrew Job 41:3). 'Who has given to God first, that He should repay?' God-owes-no-one. Pauline-grace-axiom from-OT — the closing-doxology of Rom 1-11 grounded in OT-divine-aseity.

ROM 11:1.notes: Echo of Psalm 94:14 LXX (Ps 93:14 LXX): οὐκ ἀπώσεται κύριος τὸν λαὸν αὐτοῦ ('the Lord will not cast off his people'). Paul's μὴ ἀπώσατο ὁ θεὸς τὸν λαὸν αὐτοῦ uses the same ἀπωθέω verb. Foundation-text for the 'God-has-not-rejected-Israel' argument of ROM 11.

ROM 11:11.notes: Echo of Deut 32:21 (Moses' Song; cited verbatim at 10:19) — the make-Israel-jealous theme. παραζηλόω here completes the strategy: Gentile-conversion provokes-Israel-to-jealousy, leading-to-eventual-restoration.

ROM 12:19.notes: Direct quote of Deuteronomy 32:35 LXX (with adaptation). Moses' Song. Same-text Paul cites at Heb 10:30. The 'vengeance-is-mine' formula — divine-prerogative limits human-retaliation.

ROM 12:20.notes: Direct quote of Proverbs 25:21-22 LXX. Feed-your-enemy + heap-burning-coals. The 'burning coals' metaphor is interpretively-debated: (1) shame-leading-to-repentance (Augustine, mainstream-evangelical); (2) divine-judgment-coals (minority); (3) Egyptian-ritual-of-repentance-carrying-burning-coals-on-head (some scholars). Most evangelical-readings favor (1).

ROM 12:16.notes: Echo of Proverbs 3:7 LXX 'be-not-wise-in-your-own-eyes.' Pauline-Wisdom-tradition-application to humility-imperative.

ROM 12:17.notes: Echo of Proverbs 20:22 LXX (do-not-say 'I-will-recompense-evil'). Combined with Pauline-Jesus-tradition-echo (Mat 5:38-39) — non-retaliation-ethics.

ROM 13:9.notes: Composite-quotation: Decalogue commandments 6, 7, 8, 10 (Exod 20:13-17 / Deut 5:17-21 LXX) — adultery, murder, theft, coveting — followed by Leviticus 19:18 LXX 'love your neighbor as yourself' as the summary. Same Lev 19:18 used by Jesus (Mat 22:39, Mark 12:31, Luke 10:27) and James (Jas 2:8). Pauline-axiom: love-as-Law-summary.

ROM 14:11.notes: Direct quote of Isaiah 45:23 LXX (with adaptation). Same OT-text Paul cites at Phil 2:10-11 of Christ. The 'every-knee-bow / every-tongue-confess' formula. Adds the Hebraic-divine-oath ζῶ ἐγώ ('as-I-live') from preceding-context (Isa 45:23a's λέγει κύριος + the divine-oath-formula). Pauline-implicit-Christology: at Phil 2:10-11 the same-text applies-directly-to-Christ.

ROM 15:3.notes: Direct quote of Psalm 69:9 LXX (Ps 68:10 LXX). Same Psalm 69 cited at Rom 11:9-10 (imprecation against persecutors). Pauline-Christological-application: Christ-as-Psalmist-sufferer who-bears-insults-meant-for-God.

ROM 15:9.notes: Direct quote of Psalm 18:49 LXX (Ps 17:50 LXX) = 2 Samuel 22:50 LXX. Davidic-praise-among-Gentiles. Begins the four-fold-OT-cluster on Gentile-inclusion (vv.9-12) — one each from Law, Prophets, Writings, plus a second from Prophets.

ROM 15:10.notes: Direct quote of Deuteronomy 32:43 LXX (Moses' Song closing). 'Gentiles rejoice with God's people' — Pauline-reads as Gentile-Israel-united-praise. Second of the four-fold cluster (vv.9-12).

ROM 15:11.notes: Direct quote of Psalm 117:1 LXX (Ps 116:1 LXX) — the shortest psalm of the Psalter — itself a Gentile-inclusion psalm. Third of the four-fold cluster.

ROM 15:12.notes: Direct quote of Isaiah 11:10 LXX. The 'Root of Jesse' = Davidic-Messiah. Climactic-fourth of the four-fold cluster (vv.9-12) — ruling-Gentiles + Gentiles-hoping-in-Him. The Pauline-arc: praise-singing-rejoicing-extolling-hoping in/with Gentiles.

ROM 15:21.notes: Direct quote of Isaiah 52:15 LXX (Servant-Song closing). 'Those who were not told...will see; those who have not heard...will understand.' Paul applies-as-his-pioneer-mission-warrant: preaching-Christ-where-Christ-not-yet-named.

ROM 16:20.notes: Echo of Genesis 3:15 LXX — the proto-evangelium ('your seed will-crush-the-serpent's-head'). Pauline-application: believers participate-in-Christ's-eschatological-victory over Satan. συντρίβω 'crush' echoes the LXX-Gen-3:15 verb-action.

PHP 1:19.notes: Verbatim phrase from Job 13:16 LXX: ‘ὅτι τοῦτό μοι ἀποβήσεται εἰς σωτηρίαν’ — identical word-for-word to Paul's clause here. Job speaks the line in confidence-before-the-divine-judge that his integrity will vindicate him; Paul appropriates the formula for his own imprisonment, confident that whatever the human verdict (life or death, v.20), his ultimate vindication is from God. The σωτηρία in context is broader than narrow ‘eternal salvation’ — it covers vindication + ultimate deliverance. The LXX-borrowing is one of Paul's most direct OT-as-self-frame moments outside Romans.

PHP 2:10.notes: Isa 45:23 LXX 'ἐμοὶ κάμψει πᾶν γόνυ' — Paul deliberately applies to Christ what YHWH says of himself. Same OT-text Paul cites at Rom 14:11 (where it's directly attributed to κύριος). Pauline-Christological-application — high-Christology in confession-form. The κύριος-Ἰησοῦς-Χριστός confession in v.11 makes the divine-name-application explicit.

PHP 2:11.notes: Isa 45:23 LXX 'πᾶσα γλῶσσα ἐξομολογήσεται τῷ θεῷ' continued — every-tongue-confess. Paul substitutes the confession-content: instead of confess-to-God, the confession is THAT Jesus-Christ-is-LORD (κύριος). The earliest known Christian creed-formula κύριος Ἰησοῦς Χριστός. Same allusion as Rom 14:11.

PHP 2:15.notes: LXX Deut 32:5 'γενεὰ σκολιὰ καὶ διεστραμμένη' (Moses' description of the wilderness generation). Paul borrows the word-pair verbatim but reverses the application: the Philippian Christians are blameless-children IN-THE-MIDST-OF a crooked-generation (whereas Israel WAS the crooked-generation). Lexical-pair match σκολιός + διεστραμμένη is the textual link.

PHP 3:21.notes: Echo of Ps 8:6 LXX 'πάντα ὑπέταξας ὑποκάτω τῶν ποδῶν αὐτοῦ' (you-subjected-all-things-under-his-feet). Pauline-echo: 'τοῦ δύνασθαι αὐτὸν καὶ ὑποτάξαι αὑτῷ τὰ πάντα.' Same thematic-claim that Paul makes more directly at 1Co 15:27 + Eph 1:22; cf. Heb 2:8. Allusion-not-citation: ὑποτάσσω + τὰ πάντα is the lexical-echo, but Paul is not quoting verbatim. The Christological-application: all-things-under-Christ as the eschatological-vindication of Christ's exalted-Lord status (PHP 2:9-11).

PHP 4:18.notes: Lexical-cluster echo of LXX cultic-sacrifice formulas: 'ὀσμὴ εὐωδίας' (fragrant-aroma) is the LXX-standard-phrase for accepted-burnt-offerings — appears 40+ times in Pentateuch (Lev 1:9, 1:13, 1:17, 2:2, 3:5, 4:31, etc.; Num 28:6, 28:8, 29:2, etc.). 'θυσίαν δεκτήν' (acceptable-sacrifice) and 'εὐάρεστον τῷ θεῷ' (well-pleasing-to-God) are also Levitical-language idioms. Paul reframes the Philippians' financial gift to him as cultic-offering-to-God, drawing the OT-cultic-vocabulary into a NT-stewardship context. Lev 1:9 cited as the canonical-locus though the lexical pattern recurs throughout.

COL 1:6.notes: Lexical-cluster echo of LXX Gen 1:22, 28 'αὐξάνεσθε καὶ πληθύνεσθε' (be-fruitful-and-multiply) — the creation-blessing-mandate. Paul's pair καρποφορούμενον καὶ αὐξανόμενον (bearing-fruit + growing) deliberately echoes the Gen-creation-language but applied to the gospel-message itself. The thematic move: gospel = new-creation-vitality; the gospel's spread mirrors the original creation-blessing's spread. The same pair recurs at v.10 applied to the believer's life — believer-life-as-new-creation-organism. Allusion-not-citation: the lexical-pair αὐξάνω is one of the two Gen-creation-mandate verbs; πληθύνω is replaced by καρποφορέω in Paul's adaptation.

COL 2:22.notes: Echo of Isa 29:13 LXX 'διδάσκοντες ἐντάλματα ἀνθρώπων καὶ διδασκαλίας' (teaching commandments of men and doctrines). Paul's phrase 'τὰ ἐντάλματα καὶ διδασκαλίας τῶν ἀνθρώπων' borrows the same lexical-pair (ἐντάλματα ἀνθρώπων + διδασκαλίας) — the standard NT-anti-pharisaic borrowing. Same Isa 29:13 echo Jesus deploys at Mark 7:7 / Matt 15:9 against Pharisees-tradition. Pauline-application: against the Colossian-heretics' human-tradition-based asceticism.

COL 3:1.notes: Echo of Ps 110:1 LXX 'κάθου ἐκ δεξιῶν μου' (sit at my right hand) — the most-quoted OT verse in the NT. Paul's phrase 'ἐν δεξιᾷ τοῦ θεοῦ καθήμενος' (Christ seated at God's right hand) directly evokes the Ps 110:1 enthronement-language. Same pattern: Heb 1:13, Mark 12:36, Acts 2:34-35, Eph 1:20. Christological-application: the messianic enthronement is the present-state of Christ post-resurrection.

COL 3:10.notes: Echo of Gen 1:26-27 LXX 'κατ’ εἰκόνα ἡμετέραν' (in our image). Paul's 'κατ’ εἰκόνα τοῦ κτίσαντος αὐτόν' (in the image of the one who created him/it) borrows the same image-of-Creator language but applies it to NEW-CREATION: the new-self being recreated in the image of Christ-the-Creator (cf. COL 1:15 εἰκὼν τοῦ θεοῦ). Pauline-Christological adaptation of Gen-creation-anthropology to redeemed-humanity.

EPH 1:20.notes: Echo of Ps 110:1 LXX 'κάθου ἐκ δεξιῶν μου' (sit at my right hand). Paul's 'καθίσας ἐν δεξιᾷ αὐτοῦ ἐν τοῖς ἐπουρανίοις' borrows the seated-at-right-hand enthronement language. Same Christological-application as COL 3:1. The most-quoted OT verse in the NT — also at Heb 1:13, Mark 12:36, Acts 2:34-35.

EPH 1:22.notes: Near-verbatim quotation of Ps 8:6 LXX 'πάντα ὑπέταξας ὑποκάτω τῶν ποδῶν αὐτοῦ' (you-put-all-things-under-his-feet). Paul's 'πάντα ὑπέταξεν ὑπὸ τοὺς πόδας αὐτοῦ' is essentially the same phrase. Same Christological-application as COL 3:21 (already in DB). Pauline cosmic-Christology: Ps 8's all-things-under-humanity's-feet (originally Adamic-creation-mandate) is fulfilled-and-transcended in Christ-the-second-Adam under whom all-things are subjected.

EPH 1:11.notes: Echo of LXX Deut 32:9 'καὶ ἐγενήθη μερὶς κυρίου λαὸς αὐτοῦ Ιακωβ, σχοίνισμα κληρονομίας αὐτοῦ Ισραηλ' (the LORD's portion was his people; Jacob was the apportioned-allotment of his inheritance). Pauline-application: the OT-pattern of Israel as God's chosen-portion-and-inheritance is now extended to all-believers (including Gentiles in Christ). The HAPAX κληρόω (be-allotted-as-portion) at EPH 1:11 evokes the same κλῆρος (lot/portion) lexical-family. Same theme reappears in EPH 1:14 (κληρονομία, inheritance) + EPH 1:18 (κληρονομία ἐν τοῖς ἁγίοις, inheritance in the saints).

EPH 2:13.notes: Echo of Isa 57:19 LXX 'εἰρήνην ἐπ’ εἰρήνην τοῖς μακρὰν καὶ τοῖς ἐγγὺς οὖσιν' (peace upon peace to those far and to those near). Paul's μακρὰν / ἐγγύς language directly evokes the Isa 57:19 prophecy — far+near peace-promise. The same OT-text is then quoted more fully at EPH 2:17 (the more direct quotation).

EPH 2:17.notes: Near-verbatim citation of Isa 57:19 LXX 'εἰρήνην ... τοῖς μακρὰν καὶ τοῖς ἐγγύς.' Paul's εἰρήνην ... τοῖς μακρὰν καὶ εἰρήνην τοῖς ἐγγύς is essentially the same phrase. Christological-application: Christ-himself fulfills the Isaian prophecy — peace-to-far + peace-to-near = Gentiles + Jews respectively. Same OT-text alluded-to at EPH 2:13.

EPH 2:20.notes: Echo of Isaiah 28:16 LXX 'ἰδοὺ ἐγὼ ἐμβάλλω εἰς τὰ θεμέλια Σιων λίθον ... ἀκρογωνιαῖον' (behold I lay-as-foundation in Zion a stone ... a cornerstone). Paul's ἀκρογωνιαῖος + θεμέλιος pair directly evokes the Isaian-cornerstone-prophecy. Combined-allusion-with Ps 118:22 LXX (rejected-stone-becomes-head-of-the-corner) — both texts treat-Christ-as-the-foundational-cornerstone of God's-people. Same OT-cluster at 1Pe 2:6-8.

EPH 4:8.notes: Quotation of Ps 68:18 LXX (Ps 67:19 LXX numbering): 'ἀνέβης εἰς ὕψος, ᾐχμαλώτευσας αἰχμαλωσίαν, ἔλαβες δόματα ἐν ἀνθρώπῳ.' Paul's text changes 'received-gifts among/from-men' (ἔλαβες) to 'gave gifts to men' (ἔδωκεν). The Aramaic-Targum on Ps 68:18 also has 'gave' — Paul may be following a known-Targum-tradition. Christological-application: Christ's-ascension as the basis for distributing-gifts to the church (vv.7, 11).

EPH 4:25.notes: Near-verbatim echo of Zechariah 8:16 LXX 'λαλεῖτε ἀλήθειαν ἕκαστος πρὸς τὸν πλησίον αὐτοῦ' (speak truth, each one to his neighbor). Paul's 'λαλεῖτε ἀλήθειαν ἕκαστος μετὰ τοῦ πλησίον αὐτοῦ' is essentially the same, with the only-shift πρός → μετά. Pauline-application: OT-prophetic-imperative for community-truth-telling extended to the Christian-body where members are one-another's-members.

EPH 4:26.notes: Verbatim citation of Ps 4:4 LXX 'ὀργίζεσθε καὶ μὴ ἁμαρτάνετε' (be angry and do not sin). Two-word-imperatival-pair preserved exactly. Pauline-application: anger-itself is not sinful, but anger-leading-to-sin is. The practical-extension follows: don't-let-the-sun-set-on-anger.

EPH 4:24.notes: Echo of Gen 1:26-27 LXX 'κατ’ εἰκόνα ἡμετέραν' (in our image). Paul's 'τὸν κατὰ θεὸν κτισθέντα' (the [new man] created according to God) borrows the image-of-God-creation language but applies it to NEW-CREATION: the new-self being created-in-God's-image. Same Pauline-Christological adaptation as COL 3:10 (which makes the εἰκών language explicit).

EPH 4:30.notes: Echo of Isa 63:10 LXX 'παρώξυναν τὸ πνεῦμα τὸ ἅγιον αὐτοῦ' (they grieved his Holy Spirit) — the OT's-rare reference to the Holy Spirit being grieved-by-Israel's-rebellion. Paul applies the same Spirit-grieving language to believer-sin in the NT-church. Profound Pauline-pneumatological-claim: the Holy Spirit is personally-affected-by Christian-conduct.

EPH 5:2.notes: Echo of LXX Lev cultic-formula 'ὀσμὴ εὐωδίας' (fragrant-aroma) — same Levitical-burnt-offering language used at PHP 4:18 (already in DB). Paul applies the Levitical-cultic-language to Christ's-cross as the supreme accepted-burnt-offering to God. Lev 1:9 cited as canonical-locus though the formula recurs throughout Pentateuch.

EPH 5:14.notes: Likely-paraphrase of Isa 60:1 LXX 'Φωτίζου, φωτίζου, Ἰερουσαλημ ... ἀνατολῇ ... ἥξει σου τὸ φῶς' (Be-illumined ... your light has come). Paul's 'Wake up sleeper, rise from the dead, and Christ will shine upon you' freely-paraphrases the Isa-60 dawn-prophecy applied-to-Christ. Some scholars suggest the source may be an early-Christian-baptismal-hymn rather than direct OT-citation; the διὸ λέγει intro suggests scriptural-or-quasi-scriptural-citation. Paul's-typical-formula 'διὸ λέγει' usually-precedes OT-citation.

EPH 5:31.notes: Verbatim citation of Gen 2:24 LXX. Same OT-text Jesus quoted at MAT 19:5 + MRK 10:7-8 about marriage. Pauline-application is typological: the original-marriage-creation-text was secretly about Christ-and-the-church (v.32). 'Profound mystery' — Paul reveals the OT-marriage-text was Christological-prefiguration.

EPH 5:33.notes: Echo of Lev 19:18 LXX 'ἀγαπήσεις τὸν πλησίον σου ὡς σεαυτόν' (love your neighbor as yourself). Paul's 'ἀγαπάτω ὡς ἑαυτόν' (love as yourself) borrows the OT-love-command structure but applies it to husband-wife relations specifically. The Mosaic-love-command extended to marriage.

EPH 6:2.notes: Verbatim citation of Exodus 20:12 / Deuteronomy 5:16 LXX — the Fifth-Commandment of the Decalogue: 'τίμα τὸν πατέρα σου καὶ τὴν μητέρα.' Pauline-noting that this is uniquely-the-first-commandment-with-promise. The promise-clause continues at v.3.

EPH 6:3.notes: Continuation of Exod 20:12 / Deut 5:16 LXX promise-clause: 'ἵνα εὖ σοι γένηται καὶ ἔσῃ μακροχρόνιος' (that-it-may-go-well and you-be-long-lived). Paul applies the original-Israel-promise to the Christian-readers.

EPH 6:14.notes: Echo of Isa 11:5 LXX 'ἔσται δικαιοσύνῃ ἐζωσμένος τὴν ὀσφὺν αὐτοῦ' (girded-with-righteousness-around-his-waist) — Messianic-armor-prophecy. Combined with Isa 59:17 echo for the breastplate-of-righteousness. Paul applies the Messianic-and-divine-warrior armor to the believer.

EPH 6:15.notes: Echo of Isa 52:7 LXX 'ὡραῖοι οἱ πόδες τῶν εὐαγγελιζομένων εἰρήνην' (beautiful-feet of those-bringing-good-news of-peace). Pauline-application: the believer's-feet-readiness-with-the-gospel-of-peace recalls the Isaian-gospel-bearer. Same OT-citation Paul uses at Rom 10:15.

EPH 6:17.notes: Direct lexical-borrowing of Isa 59:17 LXX 'περικεφαλαίαν σωτηρίου' (helmet-of-salvation) — God-as-divine-warrior imagery. Same OT-text Paul echoes at 1Th 5:8 (already in DB). The breastplate-of-righteousness in v.14 is from-the-same Isa 59:17 verse.
